contract.goal: Ship linear-loom plan and linear-loom retro (PLAN.md Phase 4 D2 + D3). plan is two-sided: writes PLAN.md to git AND uploads INTERVIEW.md as a Linear Document. retro uploads a single retrospective markdown file. Both reuse U1's Documents lib + GitRunner.
acceptance_criteria: cli/lib/git.ts extended with isCommitted + addAndCommit methods on GitRunner. Default runner shells git ls-files / git add / git commit. Throws git-commit-failed on non-zero exit.

cli/verbs/plan.ts: async plan verb. Refuses overwrite with plan-already-committed if target is in HEAD. Writes PLAN.md, commits, uploads INTERVIEW.md with provenance. Partial-success error names PLAN.md path.
cli/verbs/retro.ts: async retro verb. Validates --type matches kebab-case. Uploads to Linear with title <slug> · RETRO-<type>. No git writes.
cli/linear-loom.ts wires plan + retro into VERBLESS_HANDLERS.
CLI smokes: plan/retro → missing-slug; each verb's --arg-missing path reports the right arg name.
research.test.ts FAKE_GIT updated to satisfy the extended GitRunner interface (isCommitted + addAndCommit stubs).
rules_applied: DESIGN.md § 13 (provenance header — verified empirically in U1).
DESIGN.md § 5 (Documents flat under Linear Project; title-prefixed <slug> · ...).
DI continues from U1: gitRunner + readFileFn + writeFileFn + mkdirFn + now + client + markerIO + resolveAuthFn.
Mirror loom's plan-verb shape: refuse overwrite when already-committed; commit with conventional message.
disqualifiers: Touching U1's documents.ts / linear-client.ts / auth.ts / errors.ts (extended GitRunner only).
Writing back to Linear from retro beyond the upload (retros are operator-curated).
inputs: plugins/loom/cli/verbs/loom/plan.ts (structural reference for plan-verb commit pattern)

actions: Extended cli/lib/git.ts: GitRunner interface now includes isCommitted + addAndCommit; defaultGitRunner adds spawnSync calls for git ls-files --error-unmatch and git add + git commit -m; git-commit-failed surfaces on non-zero exit.
Updated research.test.ts FAKE_GIT to satisfy the extended interface (isCommitted: () => false, addAndCommit: () => {} stubs).

Wrote cli/verbs/plan.ts: parses --plan-file + --interview-file, reads marker + auth + git, refuses overwrite via gitRunner.isCommitted('projects/<slug>/PLAN.md'), mkdir + writes PLAN.md, commits via gitRunner.addAndCommit with message `[linear-loom plan] <slug>: initial PLAN.md`, uploads INTERVIEW.md as a Linear Document with provenance header. Structured success includes plan.path + plan.committed + documents.interview.

Wrote cli/verbs/retro.ts: parses --type + --retro-file, validates --type matches /^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$/ (kebab-case, no leading/trailing dashes), uploads to Linear with title `<slug> · RETRO-<type>` and provenance filename RETRO-<type>.md. Read-only — no git writes; that's archive-skill territory in U3.
Wired plan + retro into VERBLESS_HANDLERS in cli/linear-loom.ts.

Wrote 17 unit tests: 9 plan (missing args, marker-unreadable, plan-already-committed refusal, happy path with file write + commit + Linear upload assertions, provenance-in-INTERVIEW upload, partial-success error envelope) + 8 retro (missing args, kebab-case rejection for two malformed --type forms, marker-unreadable, file-unreadable, happy path with title + filename + body assertions, --pretty).
Verified: npm test → 747 passing (was 730 after U1; +17). linear-loom plan → missing-slug namespace=plan. linear-loom retro → missing-slug namespace=retro.
Panel: all four evaluators approved on first run. Tighter packet pattern continues to hold.

changes_since_previous: Second unit on the plan-retro branch, stacked behind the (now-merged) Documents-research foundation in PR #38. All five Phase 4 CLI verbs would be wired after this PR merges (research / plan / retro from Phase 4; configure / project from Phase 3). The remaining Phase 4 work is U3 — three operator-direct skill SKILL.md files.
notes_for_pr: Plan-verb refuse-on-already-committed mirrors loom's precedent. The error message names linear-loom revise-plan as the recovery path, even though that verb hasn't been implemented yet — Phase 5+ territory. v1 operators who hit this path will get a clear error pointing at the eventual remedy.

Retro --type kebab-case validation is enforced at the CLI boundary, not deferred to Linear. The substrate convention is that operator-supplied identifiers (slugs, retro types, etc.) stay in the same naming lane as everything else; rejecting `Phase_3` or `phase-3-` up-front beats a silent slugify-on-upload.

Naming-evaluator observation (not blocking): DispatchResult is now duplicated across configure.ts / project.ts / research.ts / plan.ts / retro.ts. A future cli/verbs/types.ts shared module would dedupe; punted to avoid premature abstraction with only two verbs in flight when the duplication started. Worth revisiting in Phase 5/6 when checkin / task verbs land.

Plan-verb partial-success: if PLAN.md commits successfully but the INTERVIEW.md Linear upload fails, the verb exits 1 with a structured error that names the committed PLAN.md path. The operator can either delete the commit + retry, or re-run the upload manually. No automatic rollback (matches the U1 partial-success posture).

Sandbox smoke testing (operator-side) is the validation gate for the Linear-side Document upload behavior. The unit tests cover the request structure + response handling; only a real LINEAR_API_KEY confirms Linear's documentCreate accepts our input shape.
